name: Black Garlic Honey Ice Cream
directions: Put the peeled garlic clove into the mason jar and pour the honey over it.

Loosely put the vacuum seal lid on the mason jar making sure not to tighten it. SAFETY WARNING: It is very important that you do not tighten the vacuum seal lid onto the mason jar as it can lead to the glass jar exploding.

Place the trivet in the pressure cooker bowl and place the mason jar on it. SAFETY WARNING: Make sure the mason jar is on the trivet and is in no way touching the bottom of the bowl. The direct heat can shatter the mason jar.
Pour just enough water to the bowl barely touching the bottom of the mason jar.

Cook on lowest pressure setting for 5 hours and let pressure release naturally. Pressure cook on the lowest pressure setting on your pressure cooker. The high pressure setting can leave you with a bitter after taste. Once finished, let cool to room temperature naturally.
Blend on medium high for 1 minute. Pour into a mason jar, seal and refrigerate for up to 6 months or freeze for up to 1 year.
Dry mix the sugar, dextrose, salt, perfect ice cream and reserve.
Fill the medium sauce pan a quarter of the way up with water and heat over medium high heat.
Pour the milk, heavy cream, black garlic honey and egg yolks into the bowl, add the reserved dry ingredient mixture, and blend with the immersion blender for 1 minute.
Place the bowl over the medium saucepan making sure the hot water does not touch the bottom of the bowl.
Continually whisk the ice cream mixture until it reads 185°F on an instant read thermometer, then turn off the heat.
Pass the mixture through the fine mesh strainer and refrigerate until cool.
Transfer the mixture to a Pacojet or Creami canister, freeze overnight and spin the next day.
If using an ice cream machine to churn let mixture rest overnight and churn the next day according to manufacturer instructions.

name: Reverse Spherification

ingredients.name: Flavour Liquid
ingredients.notes: This method works with Alcohol and dairy

ingredients.name: Xanthan Gum
ingredients.notes: optional, Pinch, Helps for some Alcohol or Watery Juices

directions: Add 2% Calcium Lactate Gluconate by weight to the Flavour Liquid
Pour into spherical mold and freeze
Drop frozen sphere into a warm Sodium Alginate bath (~50-60 °C)
If using Diary you can add less or potentially no Calcium Lactate Gluconate and just roll it from the spoon into the bath
These can be stored for a while, keeping them in a container with the liquid they were made from (without any gelling agents) works well

name: Snow
directions: Put Tapioca Maltodextrin in a bowl and slowly drizzle fat while mixing (food processor works well)
Sift through a sieve onto whatever
If it looks like paste or is clumpy, you need more Maltodextrin
For "soil" it's the same, but use chocolate or browned butter (can add cookie crumble for more texture too)

name: Spherification

ingredients.name: Flavoured Liquid
ingredients.notes: Whatever you want to be a spehere of

directions: Dissolve Sodium Alginate in a pan with ¼ of the flavoured liquid; Bring to simmer while whisking
Remove from heat and add in the rest of the flavoured liquid; Whisk in the Sodium Citrate; Let cool in fridge (2-4 hours or until there's no gas/bubbles)
Mix Calcium Chloride with Water when ready

Pearls: Use syringe or squeeze bottle
Spheres: Use a spherical spoon (Need to roll it in, almost like poached eggs)
The longer they sit in the liquid the thicker the skin is, pearls can get gummy quick; They will keep gelling once removed, so make only when ready to use
Use slotted spoon to remove and rinse in the filtered water

If you get tails, you're dropping from too high up.
If it's not setting add more Sodium Citrate.
